commonIssues:
Many purchase agricultural land for residential use without obtaining 90A conversion from the local authority, rendering their subsequent documents invalid.

How to fix:

  • Apply for 90A conversion online via the E-Mitra portal or Urban Improvement Trust (UIT). E-Mitra

A major challenge is plots sold by cooperative societies without proper approvals. Following government crackdowns, many cannot provide valid registration papers.

How to fix:

  • Verify if the society has a valid layout plan approved by the JDA or local municipality before buying.

While using the e-Mitra portal or Apna Khata for land records, users often experience technical issues like errors in entering Khatas.

How to fix:

  • Clear browser cache or visit an E-Mitra kiosk for assistance with the Apna Khata portal. Apna Khata

Mismatches in names, plot numbers, or boundary measurements between the sale deed and revenue records are frequent.

How to fix:

  • Apply for name or boundary correction at the Tehsildar office before registering the sale deed.

The process of obtaining Mutation (Intiqal) or a Shuddhipatra (correction certificate) for errors takes time and multiple visits.

How to fix:

  • Track mutation (Intiqal) status online on Apna Khata and escalate to the SDM if delayed.

Following amendments, registries for certain society plots were stopped, creating a crisis for those who paid but cannot get deeds registered.

How to fix:

  • Participate in regularisation camps (Prashasan Shehron Ke Sang) organized by the government to get valid leases.

Owners often find that the Jamabandi (record of rights) does not match the actual on-ground possession, causing issues with land area.

How to fix:

  • Apply for correction in the Jamabandi (RoR) through the Patwari or E-Mitra portal.

Due to poor maintenance of records, people often discover their property is under dispute or has been sold multiple times.

How to fix:

  • Always check the revenue court case status on the Rajasthan Revenue Board portal before purchase.

After a property transfer, the buyer often faces delays in having the new ownership reflected in the official revenue records.

How to fix:

  • Submit a physical copy of the registered deed to the Patwari if online auto-mutation fails.

Underestimating the total cost—including stamp duty (approx. 5-6%), registration fees (1%), and labour cess—causes financial strain.

How to fix:

  • Use the stamp duty calculator on the IGRS Rajasthan portal to budget accurately. IGRS Rajasthan

Land records are poorly maintained in some areas, creating discrepancies that require a Fard Badar or a rectification deed.

How to fix:

  • Request a Fard Badar (clerical correction) from the Patwari to fix discrepancies.

Despite digitization, not all historical data is perfectly converted, making it difficult to trace ownership beyond a certain period.

How to fix:

  • Visit the Tehsil office to obtain certified copies of old, non-digitized records.
